title: hydrocotyle sibthorpioides var. batrachium
intro: Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides var. batrachium, also known as the water pennywort, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and aiding in the treatment of various health conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, and anxiety. Hydrocotyle sibthorpioides var. batrachium can be consumed as a herbal preparation, often in the form of tea, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used as an ingredient in various topical creams and ointments.
